name: Remote PowerShell Session
notes:
This rule detects remote PowerShell connections by monitoring network outbound connections to ports 5985 or 5986 from a non-network service account.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g
SIGMA Title: Remote PowerShell Session (Network)
SIGMA Description: Detects remote PowerShell connections by monitoring network outbound connections to ports 5985 or 5986 from a non-network service account.

Confidence: LOW

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following Access.ACL Permit
when the destination port is one of the following 5985, 5986
when the event matches Initiated (custom) is any of true
when the event matches Username is any of [NETWORK SERVICE or NETZWERKDIENST or SERVIZIO DI RETE or SERVICIO DE RED or SERVICE R or SEAU]
TD_list: APPLY Remote PowerShell Session on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Access.ACL Permit
AND when the destination port is one of the following 5985, 5986
AND when the event matches Initiated (custom) is any of true
AND NOT when the event matches Username is any of [NETWORK SERVICE or NETZWERKDIENST or SERVIZIO DI RETE or SERVICIO DE RED or SERVICE R or SEAU]
RED_list: Event description: Detects remote PowerShell connections by monitoring network outbound connections to ports 5985 or 5986 from a non-network service account.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
